name: Andy Simpkins
abstract: Andrew Simpkins (April 29, 1932 – June 2, 1999) was an American jazz bassist. Born in Richmond, Indiana, he first became known as a member of the group The Three Sounds, with which he performed from 1956 to 1968.

concerts.label: Kool Jazz Festival: Sarah Vaughan and Joe Williams
JVC Jazz Festival: Sarah Vaughan and Her Trio/ Bobby McFerrin

works.t: A Little Night Music: Act II. Send in the Clowns
I Got It Bad (and That Ain't Good)

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Sarah Vaughan and Her Trio and Milt Jackson Quartet

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: And so that was the trio for the first year for the most part... Andy Simpkins stayed with her actually up until her death... at that point it was still Andy and Harold Jones still.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: so I got Andy Simpkins and Stix Hooper. And we worked up a tremendous trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Gerald Wiggins
about_quotes.evidence: Oh, now I use Paul Humphrey on drums and Andy Simpkins on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gerald Wiggins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 2, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ted Piltzecker
about_quotes.evidence: he was playing with Andy Simpkins at a place called the Matador... It was Rusty Jones, Andy Simpkins, Billy Rodgers the guitar player from Omaha, myself and George.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ted Piltzecker interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 10, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: Andy Simpkins was also in that band. Jimmy Spaulding was in that band. So we got to be friends across that time.
about_quotes.doc_title: David N. Baker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
